behaviors.description: When a required key is absent from both the file and the environment, the error message names the missing key by its exact config path (e.g., `database.host`).
behaviors.justification: Failure risk is in handoff across modules (file load + env overlay + required-key validation + error formatter), so Unit-only can miss path propagation bugs (MOCK-MISS). No real OS/network/subprocess requirement and no external round-trip side effect contract, so not System. No AI dependency, so not Agentic/Workflow. Rejected higher level because behavior text is only local config-source merge/validation, not real infra or model output.
behaviors.test_description: Run loader with a temp config missing `database.host` and with `TOOL_DATABASE_HOST` unset; assert startup fails and emitted message contains exact missing path `database.host`.

behaviors.description: When the environment variable `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set, the loader resolves the config file path relative to that directory rather than `~/.config`.
behaviors.justification: This is module interaction between env reading, path resolution, and loader file targeting (MOCK-MISS). A pure unit on path-join logic is weaker than wiring-level verification. No System-triad trigger in text (no external API contract/error contract/required externally visible round-trip beyond local wiring). No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Set `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` to a temp directory containing config; ensure loader reads that file and does not read `~/.config/tool/config.yaml`.

behaviors.description: When an environment variable with the `TOOL_` prefix conflicts with a key in the config file, the environment variable value is used.
behaviors.justification: Core risk is precedence at integration boundary between file parser and env override mapper (MOCK-MISS). Unit tests on merge helper alone may miss naming/path mapping and load-order issues. No real network/subprocess/external contract required, and no AI output dependency. Rejected System/Agentic due behavior text being local precedence logic only.
behaviors.test_description: Provide config file value and conflicting `TOOL_...` env var for same key; run loader and assert final resolved value equals env value.

behaviors.description: When the config file contains a YAML syntax error, the error message includes the line number where the problem was detected.
behaviors.justification: Need real interaction between YAML parser exception payload and CLI error rendering; unit-only formatting tests can miss parser-to-message handoff details (MOCK-MISS). No requirement for real external service/process, so System not required. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Create malformed YAML in temp config file; run loader and assert failure message includes parser-reported line number.

behaviors.description: When the config directory does not exist at startup, the loader creates it and writes a defaults file before proceeding.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires an externally visible filesystem side effect proved by round-trip observation (SYSTEM TRIAD #3). Real OS filesystem behavior (directory creation + file write + subsequent proceed/read) is part of correctness (REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE). Integration fakes can hide permission/path/creation-order issues. No AI dependency, so not Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Start CLI/loader with non-existent config root on real temp filesystem; assert directory is created, defaults file exists with expected baseline content, and loader then continues successfully using that file.
